findings.title: Stratification cutpoints are computed on the reserve-INCLUDED pool (mildly unsafe direction)
findings.detail: compute_cutpoints (draw_calibration_panel.py:410-423) runs on `pools` BEFORE the OOS reserve is removed. The reserve is only excluded later, at draw time, in draw_panel's `eligible_living` (lines 555-557). I confirmed the arithmetic: n_vol_combined=8280 = living 2142 + early 1959 + late 4179, and the 2142 living pool contains the 101 reserve names (they are delisted=0 with shares/sector/>=1500 bars). So the vol median (0.634402), vol p75 (1.062032) and cap tertiles are estimated over a pool that still contains ~101 large-cap, low-vol S&P-100/legacy names. FIX-37 states the reserve is 'excluded from the living pool BEFORE allocation' and that we 'calibrate on the disjoint remainder.' Removing ~101 low-vol names would raise every vol percentile; therefore INCLUDING them DEPRESSES the vol median and p75. Lower vol median => the living high/low boundary sits lower => the 'high-vol' stratum is systematically less volatile (the exact coarser/unsafe mechanism FIX-5 exists to prevent). Lower p75 => the >=50 tail-count gate (a hard pre-registered requirement) is satisfied with less-extreme names, weakening the tail-coverage guarantee. Magnitude is small (101/8280) but the direction is the unsafe one and it touches a hard gate; compute cutpoints on the reserve-disjoint pool (or explicitly justify the inclusion) before the calibration run.
findings.code_ref: scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py:410-423 (compute_cutpoints) vs 555-557 (reserve excluded only at draw)

findings.title: Frozen vol median/cap tertiles differ from the protocol's pre-registered values, unreconciled
findings.detail: The protocol §2.3 commits combined-pool vol median = 0.576 ('refrozen at freeze on the full pool') and cap tertiles ~$0.38B/$2.68B (§2.3 FIX-6). The frozen panel.json commits vol_median_combined=0.634402 and cap_tertiles_combined=[$0.32B, $2.15B]. Root cause: the cutpoint pool is the in-window/overlap-deduped pool (8280 names), not the protocol's literal pool (living 2142 + early 4684 + late 4179 = 11005) — i.e. disclosed deviation (a) (in-window EARLY_MIN_BARS + overlap->late) propagates into the cutpoints, shrinking early from 4684 to 1959. The manifest reconciles the pool COUNTS (pool_reconciliation, early_literal=4684 reproduced) but never flags that the resulting stratification boundaries drifted from the pre-registered 0.576/tertile values; the summary prints 0.634402 as a bare number with no comparison to 0.576. The vol shift (0.576->0.634, higher boundary) is itself in the SAFE direction, but the cap-tertile shift and the un-acknowledged propagation weaken pre-registration auditability. Add an explicit cutpoint reconciliation note alongside the count reconciliation.

findings.title: --rebuild recomputes features from the LIVE warehouse, so the frozen panel is not hermetically reproducible
findings.detail: FIX-35's stated goal is that 'a third party with the committed artifacts alone reproduces N without the exact live DB state.' But main()/write_outputs re-run build_pools + compute_features + compute_cutpoints against the live asset_prices.db on every invocation, including --rebuild (lines 845-905, 746-758). The committed frame-snapshot (calibration-v3-frame-snapshot.json) and cutpoints are written but never READ back as the draw input; --rebuild only asserts the final panel hash. Living names carry last_date 2026-06-10/11 in an actively-updated EODHD store, so once the warehouse appends new bars the realized-vol and PIT-cap features shift -> cutpoints shift -> buckets shift -> panel hash mismatches and --rebuild sys.exit(2)s. Integrity is preserved (drift is DETECTED, never silently absorbed), but the artifact cannot be re-derived from the committed files once the warehouse advances. Either re-draw from the frozen frame snapshot on --rebuild, or assert the committed warehouse_fingerprint before recomputing.
findings.protocol_ref: §11 FIX-35 (reproduce from committed artifacts without live DB state)

findings.title: Delisted equal-target tie-break systematically under-weights the most-recent (COVID-reaching) era
findings.detail: draw_delisted_sleeve allocates via largest_remainder({era|vol: 1}, 33) over 6 equal cells (line 516). With 33/6 = 5.5, three cells get 6 and three get 5, and the +1 goes to the alphabetically-first keys. Because era keys sort chronologically (dotcom<gfc<post; y2011<y2016<y2020), the EARLIEST eras always take the extra name. In the late sleeve this gives y2011_2015 6 vs y2020_plus 5 in each vol bucket (verified in the panel: late y2020_plus high=5/low=5, y2011_2015 high=6/low=6). y2020_plus is precisely the COVID-2020/Calm-B-reaching cohort the late sleeve exists to supply (FIX-4), so the deterministic tie-break mildly starves the scarce resource. Within one name per cell; deterministic, not a correctness bug — report-only. Consider tie-breaking toward the most-recent era.

findings.title: 'Early-death sleeve' contains post-2010 deaths that failed the late sleeve's 750-bar gate
findings.detail: build_pools sends a name to LATE only if is_late requires iw>=LATE_MIN_BARS (750); a name with first_date<=2000-06-30 that died after 2011 but has 250-749 in-window bars fails is_late and falls through to EARLY (lines 328-340). The panel's early sleeve 'post_2010' bucket therefore holds names that died 2010-2018 — e.g. STO (2018-05-16), HSE (2016-09-28), TSFG (2010-09-30) — which contradicts the sleeve's protocol description ('Carries dotcom/GFC deaths', §2.2). The era column honestly records the true last_date, so this is a labeling/composition-clarity issue, not a leakage or double-count bug. Note it in the sleeve description.
findings.code_ref: scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py:328-340 (is_late gated on iw>=750; else early)

findings.title: draw_delisted_sleeve has no shortfall assertion (unlike living cells)
findings.detail: draw_living_cell shortfalls are flagged in draw_panel (line 567-568: 'living cell {key}: only n/22'). The delisted sleeves have no equivalent: draw_delisted_sleeve only flags the `not room` branch (line 528). If a sleeve's entire era x vol pool were smaller than the quota, the cap-and-redistribute loop would fill every cell to capacity, `over` would empty, and it would return fewer than 33 names silently with no flag (lines 517-532). Not triggered here (early 1959 / late 4179 >> 33, and the panel shows exactly 33+33), but it is a latent gap: a future frame change that thins a sleeve could under-fill the delisted quota without surfacing a flag. Add a `len(drawn) < quota` flag mirroring the living path.
findings.code_ref: scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py:508-544 (no post-draw shortfall flag) vs 567-568 (living has one)

findings.title: Alt-seed panels skip the FIX-1 guard, so they are not apples-to-apples with the frozen panel for the K-seed check
findings.detail: Disclosed deviation (e): the alt-seed draws (lines 894-902) call draw_panel + topup_tail but never apply_fix1_guard. Their tail top-up still fills to TAIL_TARGET=55 (alt tail_topup sizes 15-25), but WITHOUT excluding FIX-1-invalid (data-error spike / nonpositive-close) names — the primary excluded 6 such names (NVOS, CETY, ALBT, ASTI, CMPC, CFIVU per the log). So an alt panel can contain names the frozen panel would reject, and its effective post-guard tail could fall below the 50 floor. Since FIX-11's K-seed check compares the (FIX-1-cleaned) primary JND against alt-seed JNDs, the alt panels are a slightly-dirtier basis than the primary. Alt panels are report-only stability INPUT (never frozen), so impact is bounded, but the approximation should be stated as a caveat on the cross-seed JND spread it feeds.

result.summary: Stratification & allocation lens on draw_calibration_panel.py vs the V3 protocol §2.4/§11. The core allocation machinery is correct and verified against the manifest: the living panel is exactly 6 (cap x vol) cells x 22 with sector spread by largest-remainder and every cell holding >=8 distinct sectors (soft floor >=6 satisfied; hard cap-4 correctly dropped); thin sector corners (<4 eligible) are taken-as-is and flagged; the delisted 33 early + 33 late split is era x vol equal-target largest-remainder (I reproduced the 6/6/6/5/5/5 allocation by hand for both sleeves); cap/vol bucket assignments are internally consistent with the committed cutpoints; the tail requirement holds (55 >= 50); and no OOS-reserve name leaks into the panel. I re-derived the eligible frame live from the warehouse and it reproduces exactly (living 2142 / early-in-window 1959 / late 4179), and the literal reconciliation reproduces the committed 4684. The largest_remainder / draw_living_cell / draw_delisted_sleeve functions have no quota-overflow, non-termination, or non-determinism bugs — the promote/demote sector-floor loop provably terminates (distinct-sector count strictly increases) and quotas never exceed availability. No blockers. The one finding with a genuine unsafe DIRECTION (rated high) is that the stratification cutpoints (vol median, vol p75, cap tertiles) are computed on the pool INCLUDING the ~101 OOS-reserve names, contrary to FIX-37's reserve-disjoint calibration — this depresses the vol median/p75, nudging the high-vol boundary and the hard tail gate the coarse/lenient way. Remaining findings are pre-registration-hygiene items (frozen cutpoints drifting from the protocol's 0.576 without reconciliation; --rebuild recomputing from the live warehouse rather than the frozen snapshot) and low-severity allocation/labeling notes (chronological tie-break under-weighting the COVID-reaching era; the 'early-death' sleeve holding post-2010 deaths; a missing delisted-shortfall flag; alt-seeds skipping the FIX-1 guard). The deliberately-disclosed deviations (a)-(e) are, on their merits, honestly recorded and mostly in the safe direction; my findings sharpen where the disclosure or direction is incomplete.

findings.title: median_pit_cap pairs RAW close with SPLIT-ADJUSTED shares → cap double-counts splits for ~69% of names
findings.detail: median_pit_cap (script lines 227-245) computes cap = raw close c × forward-filled asset_shares_quarterly.shares, with the comment 'using adjc here would double-count splits' (assumes shares are as-reported). The warehouse data falsifies that assumption: asset_shares_quarterly.shares are SPLIT-ADJUSTED (backward-adjusted to current basis). Proof (AAPL, ticker_id 17): shares show NO jump across the 2020-08 4:1 split (2020-06-30=17.42B, 2020-09-30=17.26B) nor the 2014-06 7:1 split (2014-03-31=24.6B ≈ the ~890M as-reported count × 7 × 4); meanwhile raw c DOES step at the split (2020-08-28 c=499.23 → 2020-08-31 c=129.04). Pairing split-affected raw close with split-neutralized shares therefore multiplies the historical cap by the cumulative in-window split factor. Measured impact via a faithful re-implementation: AAPL code=$2682.9B vs split-consistent adjc×shares=$322.4B (~8×); NVDA code=$698.2B vs $10.0B (~70×). This corrupts the frozen per-name cap_bucket labels AND the committed cutpoint constant cap_tertiles_combined ([$319.8M,$2152.6M]). Demonstrated bucket flips among actual drawn panel names: AFG large_liquid→mid, AMC mid→large_liquid (reverse-split case, error runs the other way). A random 400-name living sample shows 69% have |adjc/c| span >1.5 (median span 2.85×, p90 20×), so ~two-thirds of the pool is affected. NOTE on the correct fix: because shares are split-adjusted-only (dividends don't change share count) while adjc is split+dividend adjusted, neither raw-c×shares nor adjc×shares is universally exact — a dividend-only payer is correct under raw-c and wrong under adjc. The clean computation needs a split-only-adjusted price × split-adjusted shares (or raw price × as-reported shares). But the split mismatch (large discrete 2×–70× errors) dominates and the current pairing is the wrong one. SAFETY DIRECTION: not N-critical — cap is a stratification/balance axis (protocol §2.1 'nested diversity constraint, not a step-size driver'); JND fineness is driven by realized vol, which is computed independently and correctly. So this does not push the grid coarser. It is a pre-registration integrity defect: the freeze hash will lock a demonstrably-wrong committed tertile constant and wrong cap-bucket assignments that a third party recomputing 'correctly' would not reproduce, and the justifying code comment is inverted. Escalate to blocker if the cap stratification is treated as load-bearing downstream.
findings.protocol_ref: §2.3 PIT market cap / FIX-5 / FIX-6 / §11 committed cap tertiles

findings.title: Cutpoints computed on a pool that still includes the OOS reserve (FIX-37 removes reserve only at draw time)
findings.detail: build_pools and compute_cutpoints contain no reserve exclusion (verified: 'reserve' absent from both functions); reserve_ids are subtracted only inside draw_panel/topup_tail. So the frozen vol median / p75 / cap tertiles are computed over a pool that includes the ~95 S&P-100∩warehouse reserve names (large-cap, low-vol). Direction: adding large low-vol names pulls the vol median DOWN (the FIX-5-defined unsafe direction — a lower high/low boundary makes the 'high-vol' stratum less volatile) and pulls cap tertiles UP. Magnitude is small (~95/8280 ≈ 1% of the vol pool; ~95/3140 ≈ 3% of the cap pool) so the practical effect on the 0.634 median is negligible, and one can argue reserve names are legitimately part of the survivorship-free eligible population so their inclusion is defensible/correct. Reported for transparency; note it also interacts with the cap bug (AAPL/NVDA reserve caps are the most inflated names entering the tertile computation).
findings.protocol_ref: §11 FIX-37 ('excluded from the living pool BEFORE allocation')

findings.title: realized_vol filters adjc>0 then diffs, silently bridging bad-data gaps (safe direction)
findings.detail: realized_vol (lines 203-211) does pos = adjc[adjc>0] then np.diff(np.log(pos)), so any in-window bar with adjc<=0 is dropped and its neighbours are joined into a single spurious log-return. 94 tickers carry 1993 in-window adjc<=0 bars. compute_features runs BEFORE the FIX-1 guard (which only checks c>0, not adjc>0), so such names still contribute to the population vol distribution and can be drawn if c>0. Effect: a drop-to-near-zero-and-back gap manufactures a large fake return, inflating that name's vol. Direction is SAFE (higher vol → higher combined median/p75 → finer/more-conservative). Report-only; a cleaner implementation would mask non-positive bars in place (set return to NaN) rather than concatenating across them.

result.summary: FEATURES & CUTPOINTS review of draw_calibration_panel.py. VERIFIED CORRECT: realized_vol = annualized ddof=1 stdev of daily adjc log-returns × √252 (formula sound); the searchsorted ffill in median_pit_cap uses side='right' minus 1, which is the correct as-of/PIT semantics with NO off-by-one (report-date shares are used on the report date, no future leakage) and the ≥60-overlap and shares>0 / cap>0 guards are right; FIX-5 is implemented as specified — vol median, p75 and cap tertiles are computed on the COMBINED living+early+late eligible pool (n_vol=8280, n_cap=3140) with living-only (0.4728) reported alongside the combined (0.6344). DRIFT ASSESSMENT: the vol-median move 0.576→0.634 is explained by the documented pool-definition changes (the FIX-4 late-death sleeve, 4179 high-vol delisted names, was added and the early sleeve shrank to in-window 1959, so the combined pool is more delisted-weighted) and is in the SAFE direction per FIX-5 (a higher high/low boundary makes the high-vol stratum genuinely more volatile; too-LOW was the stated danger). ONE HIGH FINDING: the cap feature is mis-computed — median_pit_cap pairs RAW (split-affected) close with SPLIT-ADJUSTED shares (the warehouse shares are backward split-adjusted, disproving the code's 'adjc would double-count splits' comment), inflating cap by the cumulative in-window split factor for ~69% of names (AAPL ~8×, NVDA ~70×) and flipping real drawn-panel names between cap buckets (AFG, AMC). Because of this the reproduced cap tertiles $0.32B/$2.15B are computed on a corrupted feature, so their apparent drift from the asserted $0.38B/$2.68B is confounded, not cleanly 'explained'. The cap error is NOT N-safety-critical (cap is a balance axis; vol drives JND) but is a pre-registration integrity defect that the freeze hash would lock in, and should be fixed before the calibration run. Two low/report-only items: cutpoints include the OOS reserve names (mild, arguably-correct), and realized_vol bridges adjc<=0 gaps (safe direction).

findings.title: Freeze hash does not seal the cutpoints (nor reserve / tail_requirement / altseeds); --rebuild asserts panel_sha256 only
findings.detail: The draw is deterministically reproducible (single seeded default_rng advanced in a fully sorted stratum order; candidate lists sorted by ticker_id before every rng.permutation; seed^0x7A11 top-up substream) — that part is sound. But the FREEZE COVERAGE is incomplete. panel_hash = sha256_of(panel_list) (draw_calibration_panel.py:699) and frame_hash = sha256_of(frame) (:712) are the only two digests. The `frame` object (:701-711) contains window/pools/pool_counts/reference_counts/warehouse_fingerprint but NOT the cutpoints. The cutpoints (vol_median_combined 0.634402, vol_p75_combined 1.062032, cap_tertiles) live only in the unhashed manifest body. Because the panel entries store only DERIVED bucket LABELS (cap_bucket/vol_bucket strings) and never the cutpoint constants, editing vol_p75_combined or a cap tertile in panel.json post-freeze changes the tail requirement and the bucket boundaries that ultimately feed N, yet leaves panel_sha256 unchanged. --rebuild (:746-753) recomputes cutpoints live from the DB and asserts ONLY committed panel_sha256 == recomputed panel_hash; it never compares the committed cutpoints (or reserve, tail_requirement, pool_reconciliation, frame_snapshot_sha256) to the recomputed values, so a tampered constant passes --rebuild undetected. There is also no single top-level freeze hash binding panel+frame+cutpoints+reserve+altseeds, although protocol section 11 repeatedly references one. Fix before taking the freeze hash: emit a top-level manifest sha256 over all committed content except generated_utc, include the cutpoints inside a hashed object, and have --rebuild assert every committed hash, not just panel_sha256.
findings.protocol_ref: section 11 FIX-35 (cutpoints stored as literal committed constants so a third party reproduces N) + FIX-32 (results sha256 added to the top-level freeze hash) + section 2.4 output spec

findings.title: Plain re-run silently overwrites the frozen panel.json; write-once (FIX-32) is not enforced and generated_utc makes byte-identical impossible
findings.detail: FIX-32 pre-registers write-once: a re-run must be byte-identical or fail. The code only honors this under --rebuild. The DEFAULT invocation (`python scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py`, the exact command in the docstring/usage) takes the else path at :745-757 and calls PANEL_PATH.write_text / FRAME_PATH.write_text / ALTSEED_PATH.write_text UNCONDITIONALLY, with no existence check and no hash assertion. So a plain re-run clobbers the committed freeze artifacts. Worse, generated_utc = datetime.now(timezone.utc) is embedded in the manifest (:723), so the overwrite can never be byte-identical even when the panel is unchanged, and there is no guard to catch a case where the underlying warehouse drifted and the panel actually changed. The docstring at :32-33 claims write-once discipline the code does not implement. Fix: on a non-rebuild run, refuse to overwrite an existing committed file (require --rebuild, which asserts, or an explicit --force); keep generated_utc out of any hashed content.
findings.protocol_ref: section 8 + section 11 FIX-32 (write-once, byte-identical on re-run or fail)

findings.title: Alt-seed panels are unhashed committed artifacts and contain a FIX-1-excluded corrupt name (NVOS), weakening the draw-level stability guard
findings.detail: The K-seed alt panels (FIX-11 draw-level stability input) are drawn at :894-902 WITHOUT apply_fix1_guard, then written at :757 with NO sha256, and --rebuild never regenerates or asserts them. Concretely verified: seed 20230704's panel contains NVOS, which the primary FIX-1 guard excluded as ratio_spikes(46) = a data-error adjustment series (see summary exclusion log). The whole point of FIX-11 is to prove no seed yields a coarser grid; running that check on panels that retain adjustment-corrupt names the pipeline would otherwise drop injects non-market volatility into one seed's would-be JND and makes the cross-seed comparison not apples-to-apples. And because the file carries no hash, it can be edited freely after the freeze. The FIX-1 skip is disclosed as approximation (deviation e), but the concrete contamination plus the total absence of a hash are not neutral for the guard's purpose. Fix: apply the FIX-1 guard (or at least exclude the primary's known failures) in the alt draws, and hash the altseeds file into the freeze.

findings.title: Cutpoints are computed on the deduped in-window combined pool (8280), not the FIX-5 'combined eligible pool' (2142+4684+4179), silently dropping 966 early-death names in a plausibly-unsafe direction
findings.detail: FIX-5 (a blocker fix in the protocol) mandates the vol median and cap tertiles on the COMBINED living+delisted ELIGIBLE pool, where the eligible delisted pool is defined in section 2.2 as early=4684 and late=4179. The implementation computes cutpoints on the frame pools = living 2142 + early 1959 + late 4179 = 8280 (n_vol_combined confirms 8280). The early sleeve here is the DEDUPED IN-WINDOW pool: relative to the literal 4684, 1759 overlap names are reassigned to late and a further 966 names (total bars>=250 but in-window bars<250, verified live) are excluded from the cutpoint pool entirely. Those 966 are early-death (dotcom/GFC) names skewed high-vol; excluding them plausibly lowers the vol median/p75, shifting the high/low boundary down so the 'high-vol' stratum is diluted with lower-vol names -> coarser JND -> the exact unsafe direction FIX-5 exists to prevent. The manifest discloses the in-window-bars choice for the COUNT (deviation a) but does not disclose that the cutpoint POOL therefore deviates from the FIX-5 definition, and the safety direction of that shift is unverified. Fix: either compute cutpoints on the literal FIX-5 eligible pool, or measure and report the cutpoint shift from the exclusion and confirm it is not coarsening.
findings.protocol_ref: section 2.3 FIX-5 (cutpoints on combined living+delisted eligible pool)

findings.title: warehouse_fingerprint uses whole-table counts, not the in-window slice that determines the draw — fails FIX-35's reproducibility purpose
findings.detail: warehouse_fingerprint (:164-173) records COUNT(*) FROM prices (42,923,640), MAX(d) over the whole table (20260611), and whole-table row counts for tickers/fundamentals/shares. The draw depends ONLY on the d BETWEEN 20000101 AND 20231231 slice. So the fingerprint pins global quantities that (a) move when post-2023 bars are appended without affecting the draw, and (b) would NOT move if an in-window bar were edited while total row count stayed constant. FIX-35 asks for the row counts + last-bar date of the slices TOUCHED so a third party can confirm the exact data state that produced N; the committed fingerprint cannot do that. Fix: fingerprint the in-window slice (row count and min/max d within [20000101,20231231], per touched table).
findings.protocol_ref: section 11 FIX-35 (warehouse-content fingerprint of the slices touched)

findings.title: Summary 'reproduced now' column compares deduped in-window pools against the literal committed reference, presenting a false ~58% reproduction miss
findings.detail: write_summary builds pc = {len(pools[k])} from the post-feature DEDUPED in-window pools (early=1959) and tabulates them against REFERENCE_COUNTS (early_delisted=4684) under headings 'protocol (2026-07-16) | reproduced now' (:766-773). The manifest's pool_reconciliation actually DOES reproduce the reference exactly (verified live: early_literal_total_bars=4684, late=4179, living=2142). But the human eyeball doc shows 'early-death delisted | 4684 | 1959' side by side, which reads as a 58% reproduction failure; late shows 4179|4179, a coincidental match of a different quantity. An auditor trusting the summary would wrongly conclude the frame does not reproduce. Fix: in the summary compare like-for-like (literal reconciliation vs reference) or relabel the 1959 column as 'deduped in-window draw pool'.

findings.title: Two different vol_p75 values coexist in one manifest (rounded cutpoint 1.062032 vs unrounded tail_requirement 1.0620325)
findings.detail: compute_cutpoints rounds vol_p75_combined to 6 dp = 1.062032 (:418) and that is the frozen cutpoint; write_outputs recomputes p75 UNROUNDED = 1.0620325 for panel_tail_count (:715-716); topup_tail targets the tail using the ROUNDED cutpoints value (:591). A name with vol in [1.062032, 1.0620325] would be classified into the tail inconsistently between the frozen cutpoint, the reported tail count, and the top-up target. Harmless in this run (all give 55) but a latent off-by-one in the pre-registered >=50 tail requirement, and the reported panel_tail_count is not derived from the frozen constant. Fix: use the single frozen rounded cutpoint for the cutpoint, the topup, and the reported tail count.

findings.title: draw_panel mutates shared pool dicts (n['cell'], n['_replacements']) that persist across the primary draw, FIX-1 guard, and four alt-seed draws; primary panel is serialized only after the alt draws run
findings.detail: draw_panel sets n['cell']=key on the shared pool dicts (:561) and draw_living_cell/draw_delisted_sleeve set p['_replacements']=rest on them (:503,:541). main() runs the primary draw (:872), FIX-1 guard (:874) which mutates the same dicts (pops _replacements, sets adjustment_fingerprint, and for a failure does names[i]=sub with sub a pool dict whose cell it overwrites), then the four alt-seed draws (:894-902) which re-run draw_panel over the SAME pool dicts, and only then serializes the primary panel (:904). It is currently benign — cell is an idempotent function of the frozen cutpoints so alt-draw re-assignment yields the same value, and panel_entry never reads _replacements — but it is fragile: any future change that makes cell assignment path-dependent, or that makes serialization read _replacements, would let alt-seed call order silently alter the primary FROZEN panel. Fix: draw on per-seed deep copies, or serialize the primary panel before running the alt-seed draws.

result.summary: The draw itself is deterministic and reproducible: a single seeded numpy default_rng is advanced in a fully sorted, fixed stratum order, every candidate list is sorted by ticker_id before permutation, the seed^0x7A11 top-up substream is documented, and the committed pool reconciliation (4684/4179/1959, living 2142) reproduces exactly against the live warehouse. The disclosed deviations (in-window early counts, tail_topup to 55, implementer era buckets, alt-seed FIX-1 skip) are honestly recorded. The real problems are in FREEZE COVERAGE, not the RNG. BLOCKER: the freeze hashes cover only panel_list and the frame object — the cutpoints (which drive bucketing and the tail count that feeds N), the reserve, the tail_requirement, and the entire altseeds file are unsealed, and --rebuild asserts only panel_sha256, so those constants can be retuned post-freeze without detection; there is no single top-level freeze hash despite section 11 assuming one. HIGH: write-once (FIX-32) is not enforced — the default (non-rebuild) invocation overwrites the frozen panel.json/frame/altseeds unconditionally with a fresh generated_utc, so it can never be byte-identical and gives no protection. Plus mediums: alt-seed panels are unhashed and contain a FIX-1-excluded corrupt name (NVOS in seed 20230704), the cutpoint pool silently deviates from the FIX-5 eligible-pool definition (dropping 966 early-death names, plausibly toward coarser/unsafe), the warehouse fingerprint is global rather than in-window so it does not pin the data state that determines the draw, and the summary's reproduction table compares the wrong quantity (1959 vs 4684) so it reads as a false reproduction failure. Two low-severity items (dual p75 values; fragile shared mutable pool dicts across primary and alt draws) round it out.

findings.title: FIX-1 has no near-zero raw-price floor; sub-penny 'garbage' names enter the frozen panel and its Q=0.85 high-vol tail
findings.detail: The protocol's FIX-1 clause (a) exists explicitly to 'kill adjc/c on near-zero raw prices, e.g. LEH's $0.13 tail'. The implementation (fingerprint(), script lines 259-261) only excludes bars with c <= 0 (EXACT zero) and never imposes any positive floor on the raw close. Empirically, the frozen panel therefore contains extreme sub-penny names whose raw close bottoms out at the warehouse's 4-decimal rounding granularity (~$0.0001): MBOT/GCTK/NCPL/ATLX/INM/EPM/BNC are all present with adjustment-ratio maxima of 1,800 to 375,000 (i.e. adjc ~ $50-100 over raw ~$0.001 or less). Because 4-decimal rounding of a $0.0001 close is a ~50-100% relative perturbation, these names carry fake ±100%/bar returns, giving physically-impossible realized vols (NCPL vol=3.937, ATLX vol=2.627, GCTK vol=1.762 -> 176%-394% annualized). Critically, 7 of the 55 names in the pre-registered high-vol tail (vol >= combined p75 = 1.062) are these contaminated names (ATLX, BNC, EPM, GCTK, INM, MBOT, NCPL). Section 6/FIX-10 state the Q=0.85 estimand is governed by the top ~15% most-sensitive names, and the Q<1 guard (section 4) exists precisely because 'max is hostage to one pathological name -> grid absurdly fine -> gate vacuous'. Locking a freeze hash over a 55-name tail in which 13% are rounding-noise pathologies is exactly the vacuity mode the protocol tries to prevent, and it is the failure FIX-1 was written to catch. A positive raw-price floor (or a JND-on-log-returns sanity gate) must be added and the panel re-drawn before the freeze hash is taken.
findings.code_ref: scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py:259-261 (only c<=0 excluded; no price floor) and fingerprint() 248-289
findings.protocol_ref: COVERAGE-CALIBRATION-V3-PROTOCOL.md:34 (FIX-1 clause a, 'near-zero raw prices, e.g. LEH $0.13 tail') + :112 (Q<1 pathological-name guard) + :67 (FIX-10 tail)

findings.title: Spike-count instrument is dominated by price-granularity noise on penny names, so exclusions are arbitrary and the logged reason is misleading
findings.detail: The validity verdict is spikes == 0 (line 286), where a spike requires the relative ratio move |delta r|/r to exceed RATIO_EVENT_PCT=0.01. That 1% threshold assumes the ~1e-4 RELATIVE rounding floor cited in the code comment (lines 134-138), but that floor only holds when the raw close is O($1+). For sub-penny closes the effective relative noise is ~50-100% per bar, so the threshold is 3-4 orders of magnitude too small and the spike count becomes a random function of how rounding ticks happen to align. Reproduced directly against the warehouse: NVOS (46), CETY (265), ASTI (177), ALBT (6) and CMPC (44) were EXCLUDED for 'ratio_spikes', yet MBOT (ratio_max 16,200), GCTK (78,000), NCPL (140,000) and ATLX (375,000) -- the same sub-penny class -- PASS with 0 spikes purely because their rounding artifacts did not form the one-bar-revert pattern. Worse, the deterministic replacements the guard chose are themselves contaminated: NVOS -> MBOT and CETY -> BNC (both ratio_max in the thousands). So the guard neither cleanly removes nor cleanly keeps penny names; the exclusion log entry 'ratio_spikes(N)' asserts an adjustment DATA ERROR where the true cause is raw-price rounding, which is a pre-registration honesty problem (the fingerprint claims to certify adjustment validity but is measuring price granularity). Safety direction is indeterminate, not benign: trimming the most extreme names while keeping others of the same class perturbs the tail composition the whole gate rests on.
findings.code_ref: scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py:268-288 (spike loop + valid=spikes==0); replacement NVOS->MBOT / CETY->BNC via apply_fix1_guard 627-634; exclusion_log calibration-v3-panel.json:576-587
findings.protocol_ref: COVERAGE-CALIBRATION-V3-PROTOCOL.md:34 (FIX-1 fingerprint intended to let a skeptic confirm the convention fired correctly)

findings.title: Spike detector misses multi-bar, first-bar, and last-bar spikes, so the fingerprint over-claims 'validity'
findings.detail: Verified synthetically against the exact loop logic: a genuine one-bar spike is caught (spikes=1), but a 2-bar excursion (r=...,1,1.10,1.10,1,...), a first-bar spike (excursion on bar 0), and a last-bar spike (excursion on the final in-window bar) all return spikes=0. The revert test back=(r[i+2]-r[i])/|r[i]| with the guard i+1 < len(rel) structurally cannot see an excursion that has no pre-event bar (first bar) or no post-event bar (last bar), and a multi-bar plateau reverts too late for the immediate-next-bar check. These are real data-error classes that pass the guard. For an isolated fake return the resulting spurious disagreement pushes the grid finer (the nominally-safe direction), so this is not a safety-monotonicity violation, but it does mean the committed per-name 'ratio_spikes: 0' cannot be read as 'adjustment verified clean' -- the fingerprint's certification claim is stronger than what the code checks. The last-bar case is the most consequential because adjc is typically anchored to r=1 at the most recent bar, so a delisting-day adjustment glitch lands exactly where the detector is blind.

findings.title: FIX-1 validity rule, thresholds, and the piecewise-monotone->piecewise-constant reinterpretation are not committed to the frozen manifest
findings.detail: The protocol text specifies 'piecewise-monotone' with a recorded min/max ratio and jump count. The implementation deliberately reinterprets this as piecewise-constant-with-steps judged by zero SPIKES using RATIO_EVENT_PCT=0.01 / RATIO_REVERT_PCT=0.005. This reinterpretation and its numeric thresholds live only in the script docstring and inline comments (lines 134-145, 249-256); they are NOT emitted into calibration-v3-panel.json. The manifest records per-name outputs (ratio_min/max/jumps/spikes) and notes for the early-bars and overlap deviations (early_min_bars_note, overlap_rule), but there is no field stating what 'valid' meant, what thresholds produced it, or that the protocol's monotonicity criterion was replaced. A reviewer auditing only the frozen artifact cannot reproduce or challenge any pass/fail decision. The write-once hash does bind the thresholds indirectly (changing them changes the panel and fails --rebuild), so this is a transparency/self-documentation gap rather than a silent-tunability hole, but for a pre-registration artifact the acceptance criterion should be inside the frozen file, not only in versioned code.
findings.code_ref: scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py:139-145,249-256 (thresholds+definition in code only); manifest fields calibration-v3-panel.json:563-564 (other deviations noted, FIX-1 reinterpretation absent)
findings.protocol_ref: COVERAGE-CALIBRATION-V3-PROTOCOL.md:34 ('piecewise-monotone ... commit a per-name adjustment fingerprint')

findings.title: Guard checks c>0 and r finite but never adjc>0, so a nonpositive adjusted close passes
findings.detail: fingerprint() asserts c>0 (lines 259-261) and np.all(isfinite(r)) (264-267) but never tests adjc>0. A bar with adjc <= 0 gives r = adjc/c <= 0, which is finite and, if sustained (not a one-bar reverting excursion), produces zero spikes -> the name is judged valid with ratio_min <= 0, and the downstream fetch would emit a nonpositive Close/log-return. Currently inert on this frame (scanned all 217 committed fingerprints: min ratio_min = 0.010, no panel name has adjc <= 0), so it is a latent gap rather than an active defect, but since the frame can shift on any warehouse refresh before the freeze, an explicit adjc>0 (and r>0) assertion should be added alongside the c>0 check.
findings.code_ref: scripts/draw_calibration_panel.py:259-267 (c>0 and finite checked; adjc>0 / r>0 not)

result.summary: The FIX-1 adjustment-validity guard does not achieve its stated purpose of excluding near-zero raw-price names, and its spike-count instrument is unreliable on exactly the names it targets. BLOCKER: there is no positive raw-price floor -- only c<=0 (exact zero) is excluded -- so extreme sub-penny names (raw close ~$0.0001, at the warehouse's 4-decimal rounding granularity) enter the frozen panel; 7 of the 55 high-vol tail names that drive the Q=0.85 grid are such names, with adjustment ratios up to 375,000 and physically-impossible vols up to 3.94 (394% annualized). This is the vacuity mode the protocol's Q<1 guard exists to prevent, and it must be fixed and the panel re-drawn before the freeze hash. HIGH: because the 1% spike threshold assumes a ~1e-4 relative rounding floor that fails when the raw close is O($0.0001), the spike count is dominated by price-granularity noise -- it fired on NVOS/CETY/ASTI but passed the equally-garbage MBOT/GCTK/NCPL/ATLX, and the guard's own replacements (NVOS->MBOT, CETY->BNC) are themselves contaminated; the logged reason 'ratio_spikes' mislabels rounding artifacts as adjustment data errors. MEDIUM: the detector structurally misses multi-bar, first-bar, and last-bar spikes (verified synthetically), so per-name 'ratio_spikes: 0' over-states what was checked; and the validity rule + thresholds + the piecewise-monotone->piecewise-constant reinterpretation live only in code, not in the frozen manifest, so the pass/fail criterion is not auditable from the artifact. LOW: adjc>0 is never asserted (only c>0), so a nonpositive adjusted close would pass -- currently inert (min ratio_min=0.010) but latent. The deliberate deviations (a)-(e) are honestly disclosed and mostly safe; the core FIX-1 guard is the exception.

findings.title: Protocol text freezes 'total panel = 198'; the committed artifact is 217 (and 213-223 across seeds) — the frozen headline number contradicts its own output
findings.detail: The protocol asserts a hard frozen count in five places (§2.4 'Frozen total panel = 198 names', §3 'the full 198-panel', §8 'loads the frozen 198-panel', §8 compute note '198 assets', §11 'the frozen 198-name list'). The actual artifact is 217 (living 132 + early 33 + late 33 + tail_topup 19). The alt-seed panels are 213/217/219/222/223 — panel size is not even seed-stable, because the FIX-10 top-up need depends on how many tail names the base 198 draw happens to contain. Taking the freeze hash over a protocol that says 198 while shipping 217 defeats pre-registration: a later reader reconciling protocol vs artifact sees a contradiction, and 'select_universe() loads the frozen 198-panel / --rebuild asserts the hash' (§8/§11) is literally wrong about what is loaded. The direction is SAFE (more high-vol tail names -> finer Q=0.85 JND -> larger N) and the 217 total is disclosed in the manifest panel_size, so this is an integrity/consistency defect, not a safety-direction defect. AMENDMENT REQUIRED before freeze: replace every '198' with the top-up-inclusive definition, state the base=198 + FIX-10 top-up -> primary total 217, and note the seed-dependent size range.

findings.title: The FIX-10 tail_topup mechanism (delisted-first ordering, seed^0x7A11 substream, re-topup loop, TAIL_TARGET) exists only in code — the protocol pre-registers the requirement but not the algorithm
findings.detail: Protocol FIX-10 (§2.4/§6/§11) pre-registers only the OUTCOME ('>=50 names with vol >= combined p75'); it says nothing about what to do when the base stratified draw falls short. The implementation invents an entire pre-commit sleeve-enlargement procedure: draw from sources in fixed order ('early','late','living') i.e. delisted-first, on a seeded substream seed^0x7A11 (draw_calibration_panel.py:592), take vol>=p75 names to TAIL_TARGET=55, then a 3-pass re-top-up after FIX-1 drops (:878-884). None of this ordering, substream derivation, or loop is in the frozen protocol text, yet it materially selects 19 of the 217 panel names AND governs the tail cohort that Q=0.85 rests on. This is exactly the 'FIX-9/FIX-10 pre-commit sleeve enlargement on failure' the review flagged: growing the panel to satisfy the tail floor is directionally safe and deterministic, but 'deterministic in code' is not 'pre-registered' — the procedure must live in the protocol so it cannot be silently re-ordered before the hash. AMENDMENT REQUIRED: specify the top-up trigger, source order, substream seed, and stopping condition verbatim in §2.4.

findings.title: Era-bucket boundaries (dotcom/GFC/post for early; 2011-15/2016-19/2020+ for late) are implementer-invented and absent from the protocol — they determine which delisted names are drawn
findings.detail: draw_calibration_panel.py:127-132 hard-codes EARLY_ERAS=[dotcom_2000_2003, gfc_cycle_2004_2009 (2004-2009), post_2010] and LATE_ERAS=[y2011_2015, y2016_2019, y2020_plus], keyed on last_date. The protocol only says the delisted sleeve is 'era x vol stratified' (§2.4) and 'Carries dotcom/GFC deaths' (§2.2) — it never enumerates the era cut dates. Because each delisted sleeve draws an equal-target quota across era x vol cells, these boundaries directly change which 66 delisted names enter the panel (and thus the tail cohort). This is a free stratification knob currently frozen only in source. It does not have an obvious unsafe direction (quota is fixed at 33/sleeve; it affects composition/diversity, not N-direction), but under pre-registration it must be in the reference text before the hash so it cannot be re-cut after seeing which names it selects. AMENDMENT REQUIRED: add the exact era cut dates to §2.2/§2.4.

findings.title: TAIL_TARGET=55 is a silent +5 margin over the protocol's pre-registered '>=50' — an undocumented parameter that sets the top-up size
findings.detail: Protocol §2.4/§6/§11 pre-registers the tail floor as '>=50 names with vol>=p75'. The code sets TAIL_MIN_NAMES=50 (used only for the PASS assertion) but tops up to a SEPARATE TAIL_TARGET=55 (draw_calibration_panel.py:145, comment '50 + margin'). The manifest tail_requirement reports min_names=50 and panel_tail_count=55 but never surfaces that 55 (not 50) is the draw target — a reader checking the manifest sees '55 >= 50 PASS' and cannot tell 55 was engineered, not incidental. Direction is safe (more tail names = finer/larger N) and it is a fixed constant (no post-hoc branch), but the margin size is a real parameter that currently lives only in code. AMENDMENT REQUIRED: add TAIL_TARGET=55 (and the rationale for the +5 margin) to the §11 freeze list alongside the >=50 requirement.

findings.title: Alt-seed panels skip the FIX-1 adjustment-validity guard, and this asymmetry is disclosed nowhere in the committed artifacts
findings.detail: main() runs apply_fix1_guard on the primary panel (:874) and on primary top-ups (:883) but the K-seed alt panels are built with draw_panel + topup_tail and NO FIX-1 pass (:894-902). The altseeds JSON contains only symbol lists with no note, the summary logs only primary FIX-1 exclusions, and the script docstring does not mention the skip — so the only record of this approximation is outside the committed set. The K-seed panels feed the FIX-11 draw-level stability guard ('so no one can shop a single seed that yields a coarser grid'); running that guard on panels that can still contain ratio-spike/data-error names (the exact names FIX-1 removes from the real draw) means the stability diagnostic is computed on a different, contaminated universe than the frozen panel. The contamination tends to ADD apparent instability (mildly conservative), so it is not clearly unsafe, but an undisclosed inconsistency in a pre-registration guard is an integrity gap. AMENDMENT REQUIRED: disclose the skip in the altseeds artifact + a protocol note, or apply FIX-1 to the alt draws.

findings.title: Overlap->late tie-break and in-window early-bars refinement are disclosed in the manifest but not in the protocol text (deviations c/e-adjacent)
findings.detail: Two deliberate refinements are honestly recorded in the manifest but not in the reference doc: (1) a delisted name satisfying BOTH sleeve conditions (first_date<=2000-06-30 & >=250 bars AND last_date>=2011 & >=750 in-window bars) is assigned to LATE (draw:337-340; overlap_rule in manifest; 1759 names moved per pool_reconciliation). The protocol §2.2 defines the two sleeves with overlapping predicates and never states the tie-break. (2) The early sleeve uses IN-WINDOW >=250 bars, whereas protocol §2.2 text says 'COUNT(prices)>=250' (total); disclosed in early_min_bars_note. Both are honestly reconciled — literal_pool_reconciliation reproduces the committed 4684/4179 exactly (verified: early_literal_total_bars_no_dedup=4684, late_no_dedup=4179), and the overlap->late choice is neutral-to-safe (feeds the scarce late-regime non-survivor sleeve). Still, for the protocol text and implementation to agree at freeze, both rules should be written into §2.2. AMENDMENT: add the overlap->late tie-break and the in-window bar-count definition to §2.2.

findings.title: The realized tail is a homogeneous early-death crash cohort: all 19 top-ups are dotcom/GFC-era high-vol delistings — Q=0.85 (which the tail governs) may be set by one correlated cluster
findings.detail: topup_tail draws sources in order ('early','late','living') and the early delisted pool has enough vol>=p75 names that the need (19) is exhausted entirely from it: every top-up name in the panel carries era dotcom_2000_2003 or gfc_cycle_2004_2009 and no sector (all delisted, summary rows 199-217). The FIX-10 tail floor exists so the top ~15% most-sensitive names are well-sampled, but the finest cohort that actually sets the frozen Q=0.85 resolution is now a cluster of 2000-2009 crash-era delistings with likely-correlated death-spiral price paths. The direction is safe for N (these are the finest-JND names -> larger N), and FIX-14/17 calibration-time guards are designed to strip death-spiral/truncation artifacts, so this is report-only — but it undercuts the 'diverse tail' intent and concentrates a load-bearing part of the estimator in one era. Consider era-balancing the top-up or interleaving living-tail names; at minimum note the cohort concentration in the summary.

findings.title: Human summary mislabels the panel ('Full panel (198 rows)' over 217 rows) and presents the pool reconciliation as an apparent failure
findings.detail: write_summary hard-codes the heading 'Full panel (198 rows)' (draw:821) but then renders all 217 rows (1..217). Separately, the reconciliation table prints 'early-death delisted | 4684 | 1959' under a 'reproduced now' column (:772), which reads as a 60% reproduction MISS to a skimming reviewer; the 1959 is the refined in-window/overlap-deduped pool, and the literal 4684 is reproduced only in the manifest's pool_reconciliation.early_literal_total_bars_no_dedup (verified =4684). The eyeball doc is the artifact a human signs off on before the freeze hash, so both should be corrected: fix the '198 rows' heading to the true total, and annotate the reconciliation row that 4684 vs 1959 are literal-vs-refined definitions (not a reproduction failure).

result.summary: Adversarial pre-registration review of the frozen V3 panel draw (draw_calibration_panel.py) against COVERAGE-CALIBRATION-V3-PROTOCOL.md. The good news: the deliberate deviations are directionally SAFE and mostly honestly disclosed. The literal pool reconciliation reproduces the committed 2142/4684/4179 exactly (verified in the manifest: early_literal=4684, late=4179, living=2142); FIX-1 exclusions/replacements are logged; the top-up adds only high-vol tail names (finer Q=0.85 JND -> larger N -> more conservative gate), the draw is seed-deterministic with no post-hoc branch, and the artifacts contain only input features (vol/cap/dates/fingerprints) drawn BEFORE any calibration backtest — no gate-result leakage that could be used to retune.\n\nThe core problem is a PROTOCOL-TEXT vs IMPLEMENTATION divergence that must be closed before the freeze hash: several load-bearing decisions live only in code. Blockers (must amend the protocol doc before hashing): (1) the protocol freezes 'total panel = 198' in five places but the artifact is 217 (213-223 across seeds); (2) the entire FIX-10 tail_topup algorithm — delisted-first source order, seed^0x7A11 substream, TAIL_TARGET, re-topup loop — is uncodified in the protocol, which pre-registers only the >=50 outcome; (3) the era-bucket cut dates that select the 66 delisted names are implementer-invented and absent from the text. High: TAIL_TARGET=55 is a silent +5 margin over the '>=50' spec; alt-seed panels skip the FIX-1 guard with no disclosure in any committed artifact, weakening the FIX-11 draw-level stability guard. Medium/low: overlap->late tie-break and in-window early-bars refinement are disclosed in the manifest but not the protocol; the realized tail is a homogeneous dotcom/GFC crash cohort; and the summary's hardcoded 'Full panel (198 rows)' header + the 4684-vs-1959 reconciliation row read as errors. Net: no unsafe-direction (coarser-N) defect found, but pre-registration integrity requires the listed protocol amendments (198->217+topup rule, top-up algorithm, era cut dates, TAIL_TARGET=55, alt-panel FIX-1-skip, overlap->late, in-window bars) to be written into the protocol text so it is consistent with the implementation at the moment the freeze hash is taken.

findings.title: Sentinel/placeholder price bars corrupt the frozen realized-vol feature and misclassify low-vol large-caps into the high-vol tail (coarse-biasing = primary-unsafe, undisclosed)
findings.detail:
The warehouse encodes missing/near-zero data as literal sentinel bars (c=999999.9999 with matching inflated adjc, and c=0.0001), and the draw treats them as real prices. realized_vol() (draw_calibration_panel.py:203-211) therefore records grossly wrong frozen vols for names whose true vol is low/moderate:
- AMX (America Movil, $77B): three leading in-window bars c=999999.9999 (adjc 587488) then drops to $23 => single fake log-return of -12.46 => frozen vol=2.63; winsorizing the top-3 moves gives true vol=0.33.
- MRVL: same 999999.9999 sentinel => frozen vol=2.37, true 0.54.
- IHG (InterContinental Hotels, $7B): a c=0.0001 sentinel bar => frozen vol=2.43, true 0.33.
- FER (Ferrovial): a 428->11.30 glitch bar => frozen vol=1.13, true 0.32.
All four are frozen into the large_liquid|high cell (18% of that 22-name cell) and into the load-bearing >=p75 tail, when their true vol is BELOW the combined median (0.634) i.e. they are actually low-vol names. The FIX-1 guard (fingerprint(), :248-289) cannot catch this: it only tests adjc/c ratio spikes and c>0; a sentinel bar has c>0 and a monotone (non-reverting) ratio step, so it passes. Safety impact: seeding the sensitive high-vol tail with genuinely-insensitive names dilutes the tail's JND, biasing the grid COARSER -> undercounts N -> the PRIMARY unsafe/false-discovery direction (protocol lines 17-22), and NO downstream calibration guard (FIX-14/15/17 target artifactual fineness) corrects a coarse-biasing misclassification. It is also nowhere disclosed. The panel hash is being frozen on corrupted features.
findings.protocol_ref: section 2.3 FIX-5 (vol stratum must reflect true population); section 0 lines 17-22 (errors may only push finer); section 1 FIX-1 line 34 (adjustment-validity guard)

findings.title: IPL is outright data junk (ticker-collision signature) sitting in the late sleeve and the load-bearing tail
findings.detail: IPL (Inter Pipeline Ltd, tid=13911) has a frozen vol of 8.606 (daily sigma ~54%). The unadjusted series (adjc==c throughout, so FIX-1 sees a constant ratio and passes it) bounces day-to-day between plausible and impossible values, e.g. 2016-04-26 c=149.77 -> 2016-04-27 c=0.3920 -> 2016-04-28 c=149.04, with the five largest daily log-returns 5.95/5.94/5.75/4.94/4.90 (i.e. ~380x moves). Winsorizing the top-3 moves still leaves vol=8.19, so this is pervasive corruption (a merged/collided ticker_id), not a single bad bar. It is frozen into the late sleeve (y2016_2019, high) and is the single most extreme member of the 55-name tail that sets the finest JND. Same root cause as the blocker: the FIX-1 guard checks only adjc/c consistency, never raw-price sanity, so ticker-collision junk with a clean adjustment ratio passes unflagged.
findings.protocol_ref: section 1 FIX-1 line 34; section 2.4 FIX-10 (tail is the load-bearing estimand)

findings.title: Sub-penny / extreme-reverse-split micro-caps populate the living small|high cell and the tail; their 'high vol' is a tick-quantization artifact
findings.detail: The living small|high cell and the tail are heavily populated by sub-penny names where a one-tick move manufactures huge log-returns: NCPL (raw close min $0.001, adjc/c ratio ranges 70->140000, frozen vol 3.94, top daily logret 7.89 ~= 2665x), DFTX ($0.001, vol 2.79), ATLX ($0.001, vol 2.63), plus serial reverse-splitters whose adjc reaches absurd levels (ACHV adjc up to $398,475; MBOT up to $261,225 -- MBOT is itself a FIX-1 replacement name). Ten panel names have an in-window raw close below $0.01. At $0.001 tick resolution, realized vol is dominated by price-quantization, not economic behaviour. Effect: (a) these inflate the frozen combined-pool cutpoints (median 0.634 / p75 1.062 are computed in compute_cutpoints():410-423 over a distribution containing this junk), and (b) they pad the pre-registered >=50 tail-count requirement with non-economic vol. Direction is mostly finer (secondary-unsafe) but the cutpoint contamination is undisclosed and a hostile referee would not accept quantization noise as the basis of the finest-JND estimand.
findings.protocol_ref: section 2.3 FIX-6/FIX-7 (cap/vol cutpoints); section 2.4 FIX-10 (tail count); section 0 lines 20-22 (strip artifactual fineness before it sets the grid)

findings.title: The load-bearing tail rests on a temporally/sector-homogeneous clique of pre-2010 delisted flameouts
findings.detail: Of the 55 tail names (vol>=p75, the ~15% that governs the Q=0.85 finest-JND estimand and N): 36 are delisted and 27 of those 36 (49% of the whole tail) are dotcom/GFC-era (2000-2009) early-death names. The entire FIX-10 tail_topup sleeve -- 19 names, 35% of the tail -- is dotcom/GFC early-death, and every delisted name carries sector=None (FIX-7), so the tail is not just era-concentrated but sector-blind (many are recognizable dotcom internet flameouts: DIGX, ETRXQ, ELOQ1, OVER). The living portion of the tail is itself 37% Healthcare (event-driven biotech, a single path pathology). The finest JND -- and hence N -- therefore leans on a homogeneous clique whose representativeness for the product's actual (largely survivor, non-dotcom) trading universe is weak. Partly by design (survivorship-honest high-vol tail) and mostly finer/safe in direction, so medium, but it should be disclosed as a representativeness limit of the estimand.
findings.protocol_ref: section 2.3 FIX-7 (delisted sleeve sector/cap-uncontrolled); section 2.4 FIX-10; section 6 (effective-tail reporting)

findings.title: topup_tail implementation contradicts its own docstring ('era-balanced round-robin'), concentrating the top-up in a single sleeve/era
findings.detail: The docstring (draw_calibration_panel.py:584) states the top-up is 'Era-balanced via round-robin on a seeded permutation.' The implementation (:594-607) instead iterates sources in fixed priority ('early','late','living') and, within a source, takes a uniform permutation with NO era stratification; it exhausts the early-death pool before touching late/living. Result: all 19 top-up names came from the early-death sleeve and skew dotcom/GFC (that is where the early-death high-vol mass sits), directly producing the tail homogeneity above. This is a pre-registration-artifact accuracy problem (the frozen doc misdescribes what was frozen) and it removes the era balancing a reader would assume protects the tail. Not a safety-direction violation, but it should be corrected (either fix the code to era-balance, or fix the docstring and justify the concentration).
findings.protocol_ref: section 2.4 FIX-10; section 11 (freeze list must be re-derivable and accurately described)

findings.title: Frozen combined vol cutpoint (0.634) diverges from the protocol's committed 0.576, partly from the disclosed in-window deviation and partly from undisclosed junk inflation
findings.detail: Protocol section 2.3 commits the combined-pool vol median at 0.576; the reproduced frozen value is 0.634402 (living-only 0.472761). Part of the shift is the disclosed deviation (a): the early sleeve is computed on IN-WINDOW bars (reproduced early pool 1959) rather than the literal total-bar count (4684, which the reconciliation reproduces exactly -- confirmed), changing the combined distribution. But part is the sentinel/sub-penny upper-tail inflation flagged above. The direction (higher boundary -> finer high-vol stratum) is the safe one and the protocol sanctions 're-freezing at freeze on the full pool', so this is report-only; noting it because the delta from the pre-registered 0.576 is material (+10%) and a reader reconciling the two numbers deserves the contamination caveat alongside the in-window caveat.
findings.protocol_ref: section 2.3 FIX-5 (combined 0.576); section 11 FIX-35 (cutpoints as literal committed constants)

result.summary: Panel-quality review of the frozen Calibration-V3 asset panel (217 names). CLEAN on several axes: the pool reconciliation reproduces the committed early-death count 4684 exactly; there are no duplicate tickers or share-class twins inside the panel; the OOS reserve resolves fully (0 missing) so no reserve leakage; and the combined-median vol stratification (FIX-5) moves the boundary in the finer/safe direction as intended. The dominant problem is DATA QUALITY that the panel-stage FIX-1 guard cannot catch because it only validates the adjc/c adjustment ratio, never raw-price sanity. BLOCKER: warehouse sentinel bars (c=999999.9999 missing-data placeholder, c=0.0001 near-zero) inflate the frozen realized-vol of at least four names and misclassify genuinely low-vol large-caps (AMX true 0.33->frozen 2.63, MRVL 0.54->2.37, IHG 0.33->2.43, FER 0.32->1.13) into the large_liquid|high cell and the load-bearing tail -- which DILUTES the sensitive tail and biases the grid COARSER, the primary/false-discovery-unsafe direction, and is undisclosed. HIGH: IPL is pervasive ticker-collision junk (vol 8.61, ~380x daily moves, unfixable by winsorizing) frozen into the late sleeve and tail; and the living small|high cell + tail are padded with sub-penny/extreme-reverse-split micro-caps (NCPL $0.001 vol 3.94, DFTX, ATLX, ACHV/MBOT adjc up to $398k/$261k) whose 'high vol' is a tick-quantization artifact that also contaminates the frozen cutpoints. MEDIUM: 49% of the 55-name load-bearing tail are pre-2010 delisted flameouts (all 19 tail_topup names are dotcom/GFC early-death, sector-blind), so the finest-JND estimand rests on a homogeneous clique; and topup_tail's code contradicts its 'era-balanced round-robin' docstring (it is source-priority, exhausting early-death first). A hostile referee would reject the frozen panel until the sentinel/raw-price-sanity screen is added and the misclassified/junk names are removed or re-binned; the freeze hash should not be taken on the current features.
